records.ety: [L. dedicatus, p. p. of dedicare to affirm, to dedicate; de- + dicare to declare, dedicate; akin to dicere to say. See Diction.]

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dedicated; p. pr. & vb. n. Dedicating.]

defs: To set apart and consecrate, as to a divinity, or for sacred uses; to devote formally and solemnly; as, to dedicate vessels, treasures, a temple, or a church, to a religious use.
quotes: Vessels of silver, and vessels of gold, . . . which also king David did dedicate unto the Lord.

We have come to dedicate a portion of that field as a final resting place for those who here gave their lives that that nation might live. . . . But in a larger sense we can not dedicate, we can not consecrate, we can not hallow this ground.

defs: To devote, set apart, or give up, as one's self, to a duty or service.
quotes: The profession of a soldier, to which he had dedicated himself.

defs: To inscribe or address, as to a patron.
quotes: He complied ten elegant books, and dedicated them to the Lord Burghley.
